name: Resource Capacity Above 85%
description: Detects any resource with current capacity utilization exceeding 85%. This is an early warning threshold indicating that capacity planning should begin.
check_query: SELECT system_id, resource_type, resource_id, resource_name, utilization_pct, total_bytes, used_bytes, available_bytes FROM capacity_snapshots WHERE system_id = ? AND utilization_pct > 85 AND utilization_pct <= 90 AND snapshot_timestamp >= datetime('now', '-1 day')
impact: Resources approaching capacity limits may experience performance degradation. Without timely intervention, service disruptions could occur when space is exhausted.
remediation_steps: Review current capacity utilization trends for the resource
Identify candidates for data archival, tiering, or deletion
Evaluate whether thin provisioning or deduplication can reclaim space
Begin planning capacity expansion or procurement if growth is sustained
Set up automated alerts for further threshold breaches

name: Resource Capacity Above 90%
description: Detects any resource with current capacity utilization exceeding 90%. At this level, immediate capacity management actions should be prioritized to avoid operational impact.
check_query: SELECT system_id, resource_type, resource_id, resource_name, utilization_pct, total_bytes, used_bytes, available_bytes FROM capacity_snapshots WHERE system_id = ? AND utilization_pct > 90 AND utilization_pct <= 95 AND snapshot_timestamp >= datetime('now', '-1 day')
impact: Resources at 90%+ utilization are at elevated risk of space exhaustion. Write performance may already be degrading. Snapshot reserve space may be consumed, threatening data protection.
remediation_steps: Immediately assess which workloads are consuming the most space
Delete orphaned snapshots, temporary files, and stale data
Move cold data to object storage tier (FabricPool/StorageGRID)
Expand the resource (add disks, grow volume/aggregate)
Engage capacity planning team for emergency procurement if needed

name: Resource Capacity Critical Above 95%
description: Detects any resource with current capacity utilization exceeding 95%. Resources at this level are at imminent risk of space exhaustion and require emergency action.
check_query: SELECT system_id, resource_type, resource_id, resource_name, utilization_pct, total_bytes, used_bytes, available_bytes FROM capacity_snapshots WHERE system_id = ? AND utilization_pct > 95 AND snapshot_timestamp >= datetime('now', '-1 day')
impact: Imminent risk of write failures, application outages, and data loss. ONTAP aggregates above 95% trigger automatic volume offline behavior. Snapshots may be auto-deleted.
remediation_steps: EMERGENCY: Take immediate action to free space or expand capacity
Delete non-critical snapshots and FlexClone volumes
Move or delete temporary and orphaned data immediately
If ONTAP: check for vol autosize and guarantee settings
Expand aggregate/pool with available spare disks
Contact NetApp Support if unable to free sufficient space
Notify application owners of potential service impact
support_case_note: Open a P1 support case if capacity cannot be freed and the resource is expected to fill within 24 hours.

name: Growth Rate Exceeds 5% Per Week
description: Detects resources where the weekly growth rate exceeds 5% of total capacity, indicating unusually rapid data accumulation that may require investigation.
check_query: SELECT cp.system_id, cp.resource_type, cp.resource_id, cp.resource_name, cp.growth_rate_bytes_per_day, cp.days_until_full, cs.total_bytes, ROUND((cp.growth_rate_bytes_per_day * 7.0 / NULLIF(cs.total_bytes, 0)) * 100, 2) AS weekly_growth_pct FROM capacity_projections cp JOIN capacity_snapshots cs ON cp.system_id = cs.system_id AND cp.resource_type = cs.resource_type AND cp.resource_id = cs.resource_id WHERE cp.system_id = ? AND cs.snapshot_timestamp >= datetime('now', '-1 day') AND (cp.growth_rate_bytes_per_day * 7.0 / NULLIF(cs.total_bytes, 0)) > 0.05 GROUP BY cp.system_id, cp.resource_type, cp.resource_id
impact: Rapid growth may indicate backup sprawl, snapshot accumulation, runaway application writes, or data migration overload. Left unchecked, the resource could fill sooner than projected.
remediation_steps: Investigate the source of rapid data growth (applications, backups, snapshots)
Check for snapshot policy misconfiguration or excessive retention
Review backup schedules and retention policies
Verify no unintended data replication or migration is in progress
Adjust growth projections and update capacity planning forecasts
Consider implementing volume autosize with appropriate limits

name: Resource Full Within 30 Days
description: Detects resources projected to reach full capacity within 30 days at the current growth rate. Immediate capacity planning and execution are required.
check_query: SELECT system_id, resource_type, resource_id, resource_name, days_until_full, growth_rate_bytes_per_day, current_used_bytes, projected_full_date FROM capacity_projections WHERE system_id = ? AND days_until_full > 0 AND days_until_full <= 30
impact: The resource will be completely full within one month. Without intervention, write failures, application outages, and potential data loss will occur at the projected full date.
remediation_steps: Begin emergency capacity expansion procedures
Identify and execute quick-win space reclamation (snapshot cleanup, temp data deletion)
Expedite procurement of additional storage if expansion is not possible with existing resources
Implement data lifecycle policies (tiering, archival, purging)
Notify stakeholders of the projected full date and planned remediation
Consider implementing quotas to limit growth from specific workloads
support_case_note: Open a proactive support case if the resource is projected to fill within 7 days and internal remediation cannot keep pace.

name: Resource Full Within 90 Days
description: Detects resources projected to reach full capacity within 90 days (but more than 30 days). Include these resources in the next capacity planning review cycle.
check_query: SELECT system_id, resource_type, resource_id, resource_name, days_until_full, growth_rate_bytes_per_day, current_used_bytes, projected_full_date FROM capacity_projections WHERE system_id = ? AND days_until_full > 30 AND days_until_full <= 90
impact: The resource is on track to fill within one quarter. There is sufficient time for planned capacity expansion, but action must be taken in the current planning cycle to avoid escalation to critical.
remediation_steps: Include this resource in the next capacity planning review meeting
Evaluate whether data lifecycle management can slow growth sufficiently
Begin procurement process for additional capacity if needed
Review growth trend accuracy and adjust projections as needed
Consider implementing FabricPool tiering for cold data

name: Snapshot Space Exceeds 200% of Reserve
description: Detects volumes where snapshot space consumption exceeds 200% of the configured snapshot reserve, spilling into the active filesystem space and reducing available capacity.
check_query: SELECT v.system_id, 'volume' AS resource_type, v.name AS resource_id, v.name AS resource_name, v.snapshot_reserve_pct, SUM(s.size_bytes) AS total_snapshot_bytes, v.size_bytes, ROUND(CAST(SUM(s.size_bytes) AS REAL) / NULLIF((v.size_bytes * v.snapshot_reserve_pct / 100.0), 0) * 100, 1) AS snapshot_reserve_usage_pct FROM ontap_volumes v JOIN ontap_snapshots s ON s.volume_id = v.id WHERE v.system_id = ? AND v.state = 'online' AND v.type = 'rw' AND v.snapshot_reserve_pct > 0 GROUP BY v.id HAVING snapshot_reserve_usage_pct > 200
impact: Snapshot space overflow consumes active filesystem capacity, reducing usable space for applications. This can lead to unexpected out-of-space conditions and write failures.
remediation_steps: Review snapshot policies for excessive retention periods
Delete old or unnecessary snapshots that are consuming space
Increase the snapshot reserve percentage if the workload requires more snapshots
Check for locked snapshots (SnapMirror, SnapVault) that cannot be deleted
Consider enabling volume autosize to accommodate snapshot growth
Review application write patterns that may cause excessive snapshot delta

name: Deduplication/Compression Ratio Declining
description: Detects resources where the logical-to-physical data ratio (deduplication and compression efficiency) has declined compared to previous measurements, indicating reduced storage efficiency.
check_query: SELECT cs1.system_id, cs1.resource_type, cs1.resource_id, cs1.resource_name, ROUND(CAST(cs1.total_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(cs1.used_bytes, 0), 2) AS current_ratio, ROUND(CAST(cs2.total_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(cs2.used_bytes, 0), 2) AS previous_ratio FROM capacity_snapshots cs1 JOIN capacity_snapshots cs2 ON cs1.system_id = cs2.system_id AND cs1.resource_type = cs2.resource_type AND cs1.resource_id = cs2.resource_id WHERE cs1.system_id = ? AND cs1.snapshot_timestamp >= datetime('now', '-1 day') AND cs2.snapshot_timestamp >= datetime('now', '-8 days') AND cs2.snapshot_timestamp < datetime('now', '-6 days') AND cs1.used_bytes > 0 AND cs2.used_bytes > 0 AND (CAST(cs1.total_bytes AS REAL) / cs1.used_bytes) < (CAST(cs2.total_bytes AS REAL) / cs2.used_bytes) * 0.85
impact: Declining deduplication/compression ratios mean physical capacity is being consumed faster than expected. This accelerates time-to-full and may indicate changes in data patterns.
remediation_steps: Investigate whether new workloads with incompressible data have been provisioned
Verify that inline and post-process deduplication/compression are enabled
Run a manual deduplication scan if post-process dedup is used
Check for data pattern changes (encrypted data, pre-compressed content)
Update capacity projections to account for the reduced efficiency
Consider adaptive compression settings for mixed workloads

name: Capacity Utilization Imbalanced Across Resources
description: Detects when capacity utilization varies by more than 20 percentage points across resources of the same type within a system, indicating potential load or provisioning imbalance.
check_query: SELECT system_id, resource_type, MIN(utilization_pct) AS min_util, MAX(utilization_pct) AS max_util, ROUND(AVG(utilization_pct), 1) AS avg_util, ROUND(MAX(utilization_pct) - MIN(utilization_pct), 1) AS variance_pct, COUNT(*) AS resource_count FROM capacity_snapshots WHERE system_id = ? AND snapshot_timestamp >= datetime('now', '-1 day') GROUP BY system_id, resource_type HAVING resource_count > 1 AND variance_pct > 20
impact: Imbalanced utilization means some resources may hit capacity limits while others have significant free space. This leads to inefficient use of purchased capacity and premature expansion needs.
remediation_steps: Review volume/LUN placement across aggregates or pools
Rebalance workloads by moving volumes to less-utilized resources
Use FlexGroup or storage pool features for automatic balancing
Investigate whether specific workloads are disproportionately consuming space
Consider implementing provisioning policies to enforce balanced placement

name: No Capacity Data Collected in 48+ Hours
description: Detects systems where no capacity snapshot data has been collected in the last 48 hours, indicating a potential polling failure, connectivity issue, or configuration problem.
check_query: SELECT s.id AS system_id, s.name AS resource_name, 'system' AS resource_type, s.name AS resource_id, s.last_polled, ROUND((julianday('now') - julianday(s.last_polled)) * 24, 1) AS hours_since_poll FROM systems s WHERE s.id = ? AND (s.last_polled IS NULL OR s.last_polled < datetime('now', '-48 hours')) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM capacity_snapshots cs WHERE cs.system_id = s.id AND cs.snapshot_timestamp >= datetime('now', '-48 hours'))
impact: Without current capacity data, trend analysis, projections, and alerting are unreliable. Capacity issues may go undetected until they cause service impact.
remediation_steps: Verify the system is reachable and responding to API calls
Check the data collection service status and logs for errors
Validate API credentials have not expired or been revoked
Restart the collection service if it has stalled
Check network connectivity between the collector and the storage system
Verify the polling schedule configuration is correct
